meaning: <美>n.(前自由派在政治上转向保守的) 新保守主义者,新保守派
examples: I'm a conservative, but I'm certainly not a neoconservative.
"Neoconservative", which appeared in 1973, was not his coining.
First, wielding power abroad proved harder in practice than in neoconservative theory.
Well, first, consider whether Robert Gates's arrival is really a repudiation of the so-called neoconservative vision.
The neoconservative is often cited as one of the main architects of the Iraq war.
